title: Product & Marketing Analyst (all genders) – Stage+ – Deutsche Grammophon GmbH
required_skills: Analytical and problem-solving skills (large data sets, trend identification)
2-3 years experience in product analytics and marketing analytics
Bachelor's degree in Marketing Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, or Business Administration
Google Tag Manager, Mixpanel, Adjust, data visualization tools
Finance basics to match performance data to business metrics
matched_skills: Analytical and problem-solving skills with large data sets
Marketing analytics experience (4+ years across e-commerce, FinTech, manufacturing)
Finance basics (financial analytics for USD 8M programme, budget dashboards)
Cross-functional team coordination and project management (Scrum, SAFe)
Campaign performance optimisation (maps to marketing campaign ROI analysis)
Marketing KPI framework design and dashboards
Data quality, tracking and QA (maps to tracking concepts requirement)
missing_skills: Google Tag Manager (not listed in CV)
Product analytics in a platform/app/subscription environment
core_responsibilities: Analyze marketing campaigns and product data to evaluate ROI, identify improvement areas, and recommend optimizations
Develop and implement tracking concepts for website and mobile/TV apps
Conduct A/B tests to optimize user experience on Stage+ and marketing campaign performance across channels
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports tracking Stage+ KPIs
Support product development process by defining success criteria and conducting hypothesis-driven analyses
recommended_emphasis: Highlight A/B testing and experiment design experience explicitly
Emphasize campaign ROI analytics and marketing performance measurement
Showcase dashboard and KPI reporting work (executive dashboards for 12 product lines at Minth, budget dashboards at HSBC)
Frame data quality and tracking QA work at Hapag-Lloyd as relevant to tracking implementation
Position multi-market analytics experience (EMEA, APAC) as consumer behaviour insight capability
Mention any exposure to digital tools (Google Analytics, Tag Manager equivalents) even if indirect
Stress cross-functional collaboration with product and marketing teams
